items.title: We go hands-on with Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and its 5X and 6X multipliers — more generated frames, now tailor-made for your monitor's refresh rate
items.description: The arrival of Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Generation mode and its extended 5X and 6X multipliers promises more control and higher generated frame rates for GeForce RTX 50-series graphics cards. We went hands-on to see just how far AI can stretch one input frame.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter For GeForce gaming, 2026 has so far been the year of software updates to existing GPUs as the world's cutting-edge silicon gets swallowed up by AI demand. DLSS 4.5 upscaling was the first of those updates to arrive all the way back at CES 2026. That new upscaling model runs best on RTX 50- and 40-series GPUs, but it technically works with graphics cards stretching all the way back to the RTX 20-series family if you’re willing to tolerate a performance hit.The second new piece of the software-defined performance puzzle, Dynamic Multi Frame Generation (MFG) with 5X and 6X modes, arrives today as part of a beta update for the Nvidia App, and it’s exclusively for RTX 50-series cards. We’ve been playing with the tech to determine whether even more generated frames can make for a better gaming experience, and at what cost, if any, it has for critical measures of responsiveness.As a quick refresher, MFG so far has offered fixed multipliers of 3X or 4X in addition to the baseline 2X framegen introduced with RTX 40-series cards. Because those options are static, they don’t account for the changing demands of gaming workloads, and it’s easy to end up straddling a line where your graphics card is generating output frame rates above or below your monitor’s peak refresh rate. Neither situation is ideal, but until now, finding the multiplier that splits the difference well enough has been the only way to run MFG.Article continues below You may like Nvidia DLSS 4.5 Dynamic MFG plus 5X and 6X framegen modes enter beta for RTX 50-series users Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Dynamic MFG comes to RTX 50-series GPUs March 31 Dynamic MFG takes a step toward smoothing out that awkwardness. Give it a target frame rate, and the tech can automatically shift multipliers on the fly to maintain it. The new 5X and 6X modes can be used statically if you wish, but they’re better understood as additional gears that let Dynamic MFG work at its best. Or at least that’s the promise. Whether Nvidia’s MFG AI model can conjure up four or five additional frames from just one input frame’s worth of data with acceptable latency is the pressing question that’s been facing us for the past few days.The setupThe first step in getting Dynamic MFG running starts with the latest Nvidia App. Once installed alongside the latest 595.97 driver release, the app should expose a global Frame Generation Mode override in its graphics settings, from which you can force Dynamic mode along with a target frame rate and, optionally, a maximum multiplier cap. Toggle Dynamic mode to on, enter your target frame rate (typically your monitor's maximum refresh rate), and the foundation is laid.However, simply toggling this global setting doesn’t mean Dynamic MFG will work with every game. Nvidia provides a list of supported titles to play with (filter by 6X in the MFG column for the relevant results), but in the meantime, the Nvidia App will tell you whether a game is compatible with your global Dynamic MFG override or not right in its settings list.With this global toggle enabled, simply turning on MFG in compatible games should generally let Dynamic MFG take over. With the latest Nvidia App version launching today, you can confirm that your DLSS MFG, upscaling, and ray reconstruction settings are all working as expected by pressing Alt + Z to invoke the Nvidia overlay before calling up the most verbose reporting mode it offers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsSmoothness, responsiveness, and image qualityThe first test for Dynamic MFG is whether its shifts between multipliers cause any hitches or stutters during gameplay. Happily, I didn’t see any such unevenness as Dynamic MFG did its thing. You can always see the multiplier shift in the Nvidia App performance overlay, of course, but without that visual confirmation, it might as well be seamless. Whatever variance might occur during these mode shifts seems to be smoothed over by a variable-refresh-rate display like our testbed’s monitor.That leads us to the output image quality of MFG’s new 5X and 6X modes. Despite the warranted skepticism one might have about stretching one frame’s worth of input data into four or five extra frames, my initial impression is that MFG’s new 5X and 6X modes do work, and they work well enough that they’re mostly unnoticeable in practice. In other words, it’s remarkable that they’re unremarkable. (Image credit: Future)I say mostly because you can still see some potential artifacting from MFG in some games if you’re looking for it. The worst examples I’ve seen so far are in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, where I noticed that the edge of Gustave’s waving coat can leave ghostly trails behind it, and player shadows start to get “noisy” or less solid at the edges than they ought to be in motion. (Enlarge the image above to see the first issue.) These issues are only minor with MFG 4X, but they become slightly more obvious with MFG 5X and 6X. What to read next We got a first look at Nvidia's DLSS 5 and the future of neural rendering at GTC Nvidia debuts DLSS 5 for hyper-realistic visual fidelity in games Community tests confirm DLSS 4.5 yields 20%+ performance loss on older RTX 30 and 20 series GPUs compared to DLSS 4.0 Hogwarts Legacy and Cyberpunk 2077 also show a bit of ghosting or raggedness at the bottom edge of the screen in motion with MFG enabled, but it’s milder and easy enough to overlook since it’s well outside of the typical focus point for such games (i.e., the center of the screen).Looking beyond those minor image quality concerns, the new combination of MFG multipliers up to 6X and the upscaling prowess of DLSS 4.5’s improved models opens up high-refresh-rate experiences at settings that would previously have been difficult to realize on lower-end graphics cards.As an extreme example, MFG 6X and DLSS 4.5 Ultra Performance with Preset L makes Cyberpunk 2077 totally playable at a 240 FPS target on even the RTX 5070 with ultra settings and path tracing enabled, all with an input latency of about 52 ms.We also measured PC latency with framegen off, at 2X, at 4X, and at a forced 6X to see whether the extra frames incur extra input latency, but in line with Nvidia’s claims, PC latency (as measured by the FrameView app) remained largely the same as with 4X.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCyberpunk 2077 input latency: RTX 5070, DLSS Ultra Performance, 4K target resolution, path tracing onMFG multiplierInput latency (ms)Off35.02X46.64X53.26X52.6However, performance scaling across the various modes didn’t track as cleanly with those multipliers as we would have expected. In another round of testing with our RTX 5080 in Cyberpunk 2077, the average frame rates captured by FrameView with MFG active all fall short of the lofty figures you might expect from each multiplier.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCyberpunk 2077 performance: RTX 5080, DLSS Performance, 4K target resolution, path tracing onMFG multiplierAverage FPS Off 60.02X 103.14X178.86X 247.7As you can see from the figures above, despite our 60 FPS-ish baseline with DLSS Performance and a 4K target resolution, adding MFG to the picture didn’t result in the full boost you would expect, even with such a powerful graphics card in play. We’re investigating the reasons behind this shortfall and will update this article with further details as we’re able.All that said, unlike dynamic resolution (or upscaling) modes that noticeably sacrifice image quality to maintain frame rates, Dynamic MFG mostly preserves the highest practical image quality from a given game and graphics card while transparently adjusting output frame rates as needed.It’s the closest a dynamic performance mode has ever come to letting me stop worrying about output FPS entirely while gaming, and it’s another clear step into a future where output frame rate is just another setting to tweak instead of a hard limit of silicon.Bottom line and what’s next If you’re already sold on what MFG has to offer, the combination of Dynamic MFG and its expanded multipliers makes framegen a set-and-forget experience instead of a . Enable Dynamic MFG globally and dial in your monitor’s peak refresh rate as your frame rate target in the Nvidia App, choose the in-game quality settings that produce a level of input latency you’re comfortable with, enable MFG in-game, and let Dynamic MFG take over.That’s a fair amount of setup, but once it’s done, you’ll never have to worry about micromanaging your MFG multiplier to stay near the peak of your display’s refresh rate range ever again. It’s a great refinement of this tech, and we didn’t see any hitches or stutters as a result of multiplier shifts during gameplay.The bigger question mark for many gamers is whether Nvidia can successfully generate even more frames from the same limited input data that MFG already ingests. As wild as stretching a single frame’s worth of input data into four or five generated frames may sound, MFG’s new 5X and 6X modes work well in our experience so far, and they work without introducing new or worse visual artifacts than we’ve already seen from past versions of the tech.We also didn’t measure a large increase to input latency in exchange for the extra frames, which we’re glad for as Nvidia leans harder and harder on neural rendering techniques like MFG to enhance perceived performance.If you haven’t tried MFG yet, and you already have a Blackwell GPU and a sufficiently high-refresh-rate monitor at your disposal, Dynamic MFG and its 5X and 6X modes are well worth a shot. You have nothing to lose, and if it’s not to your liking, you can turn it off again. If you still insist that every pixel of every frame be natively shaded at 4K, well, Nvidia has a graphics card for you, and it’s called the RTX 5090.Joking aside, and as our past testing has already demonstrated, MFG still isn’t a performance panacea. It can only work off the baseline experience a given graphics card can offer, whether for better or for worse. It doesn’t let a weaker graphics card run more demanding settings than it otherwise could (which is still the domain of DLSS upscaling).If you crank settings too high on too weak a GPU before adding framegen to the picture, your input latency will still rise beyond the baseline you need for a playable experience, and all the new MFG bells and whistles launching today don’t change that.That leads us to another important point: If you’re still running a 1080p or 60Hz monitor (or worse, a 1080p and 60 Hz monitor) alongside any 12GB or 16GB Blackwell GPU that can capably run MFG, it’s time to consider an upgrade.Especially with the advent of the DLSS 4.5 upscaling model and the fidelity it offers in its Performance and Ultra Performance presets, a high-refresh-rate 1440p or 4K monitor no longer requires a top-of-the-line graphics card to achieve a fluid and responsive experience with high image quality. Any 1080p monitor, however, puts a hard cap on upscaling image quality, and a 60 Hz monitor of any resolution puts a hard cap on fluid motion and (to a degree) responsiveness.If you’re still running such a setup and have the means to upgrade to a higher-resolution, higher-refresh-rate display, there’s never been a better time amid spiraling costs for basics like RAM and SSDs. Monitors remain relatively insulated from the RAMpocalypse and NANDpocalypse, so if you’re pondering what your next upgrade should be, check out our list of the best gaming monitors for inspiration and put Dynamic MFG to work maxing out their refresh rates.
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: 我们亲身体验英伟达DLSS 4.5动态多帧生成技术及其5倍、6倍增帧效果——生成更多画面帧，现可为显示器刷新率量身打造。
items.descriptionZh:
2026年，在尖端AI算力需求吞噬大量先进制程芯片的背景下，GeForce游戏显卡的演进更多地依赖于对现有硬件的软件优化。继年初CES上发布DLSS 4.5超分辨率技术后，英伟达于3月31日通过Nvidia App的测试版更新，推出了名为“动态多帧生成”（Dynamic Multi Frame Generation，简称Dynamic MFG）的关键软件升级，并首次引入了5X和6X帧生成模式。这项技术目前为RTX 50系列（Blackwell架构）显卡独占。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
多帧生成（MFG）技术自RTX 40系列引入，通过AI模型基于前后游戏帧和运动数据，在原始帧之间插入全新生成的帧，从而倍增帧率。此前，MFG提供固定的2X、3X或4X倍数选项。然而，固定倍数无法适应游戏负载的动态变化，容易导致输出帧率与显示器刷新率不匹配，造成资源浪费或画面撕裂。

动态MFG的核心创新在于其**自适应能力**。用户设定一个目标帧率（通常为显示器最高刷新率），该技术便能实时、自动地在不同的MFG倍数（如3X、4X、5X、6X）之间切换，以尽可能稳定地维持目标帧率。新增的5X和6X模式既可作为静态选项使用，但更重要的意义是作为动态MFG“工具箱”中的额外档位，为其在更广泛的性能区间内实现平滑适配提供了可能。其技术挑战在于，AI模型能否仅凭单帧输入数据，可靠地生成多达四或五个额外帧，同时保持可接受的延迟和图像质量。

**性能表现与实测分析**
根据Tom's Hardware的实测，动态MFG在游戏过程中切换倍数时**未引发可感知的卡顿或掉帧**，得益于可变刷新率（VRR）显示器的补偿，模式切换近乎无缝。

在图像质量方面，尽管存在理论上的顾虑，但5X和6X模式在实际游戏中**表现令人惊讶地可靠**，大多数情况下难以察觉。只有在特意观察时，才能在部分游戏（如《Clair Obscur: Expedition 33》、《霍格沃茨遗产》、《赛博朋克2077》）中看到些许重影或边缘噪点，且这些瑕疵在6X模式下比4X模式略为明显，但通常位于画面边缘或非焦点区域，对整体体验影响有限。

性能提升与延迟是测试重点。在《赛博朋克2077》中，启用路径追踪和DLSS超高性能模式后，即使RTX 5070也能借助MFG 6X实现240 FPS的目标帧率，输入延迟约为52毫秒。关键的是，**输入延迟并未随生成帧数增加而显著上升**。测试数据显示，从MFG关闭到启用6X，输入延迟增长相对平缓，且4X与6X模式的延迟几乎相同。

| MFG倍数 | 输入延迟 (ms) - RTX 5070 |
| :--- | :--- |
| 关闭 | 35.0 |
| 2X | 46.6 |
| 4X | 53.2 |
| 6X | 52.6 |

然而，实测的**平均帧率提升并未完全达到理论倍数**。例如，RTX 5080在特定设置下，基线帧率约60 FPS，启用MFG 6X后平均帧率为247.7 FPS，而非预期的360 FPS左右。这表明性能缩放存在瓶颈，具体原因有待进一步探究。

| MFG倍数 | 平均帧率 (FPS) - RTX 5080 |
| :--- | :--- |
| 关闭 | 60.0 |
| 2X | 103.1 |
| 4X | 178.8 |
| 6X | 247.7 |

**技术影响与应用场景**
动态MFG结合DLSS 4.5，标志着游戏性能管理向更智能、更“无感”的方向迈进了一大步。其核心影响在于：
1.  **化繁为简的体验**：对于接受MFG技术的用户，动态MFG使其成为“设置后即可忘记”的功能。用户只需全局设定目标刷新率，然后在游戏中开启MFG，无需再手动调整倍数来匹配显示器。
2.  **释放中端显卡潜力**：这使得高端高刷新率体验（如4K 240Hz）在RTX 5070等中高端显卡上，通过画质妥协（使用DLSS性能模式）成为可能，降低了高帧率游戏的门槛。
3.  **明确技术边界与依赖**：MFG并非万能。它不能弥补GPU原生性能的不足。如果基础帧率过低或设置过高导致原生延迟巨大，MFG无法创造可玩的体验。此外，**显示设备成为新瓶颈**：要充分发挥动态MFG和DLSS 4.5的潜力，用户需要一台高刷新率（至少144Hz）、高分辨率（1440p或4K）的显示器。1080p分辨率会限制超分技术的画质上限，而60Hz刷新率则完全限制了高帧率的用武之地。

**总结与建议**
动态MFG及其5X/6X模式是英伟达神经网络渲染技术的一次重要精进。它在未明显增加输入延迟或引入严重画质损失的前提下，提供了更智能、更自适应的帧率提升方案，显著改善了高刷新率游戏的易用性和体验平滑度。

对于已拥有RTX 50系列显卡和高刷新率显示器的玩家，强烈建议尝试开启动态MFG。对于仍在使用1080p/60Hz显示器的用户，若想体验这些前沿技术带来的流畅度飞跃，**优先升级显示器**比升级显卡更为紧迫和有效。在当前存储硬件（RAM、SSD）价格波动的背景下，显示器的性价比相对稳定，是提升整体游戏体验的关键一环。

items.title: The Galaxy S26’s photo app can sloppify your memories
items.description:
Love to see the BACKSST BOYS at the Sphere.	

The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run.
Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with changes to the background - make the sky more blue, or remove crowds of tourists. Things got weird once the company added natural language requests and let you ask for basically any change. There were some guardrails, but in many cases it was easy to prompt your way around them into creating a potentially harmful image of something that never happened - helicopter crashes, smoking bombs on street corners, that kind of thing. 
That's the world Samsung's updated Photo Assist steps into. At Unpacked in Febru …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run.Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with changes to the background — make the sky more blue, or remove crowds of tourists. Things got weird once the company added natural language requests and let you ask for basically any change. There were some guardrails, but in many cases it was easy to prompt your way around them into creating a potentially harmful image of something that never happened — helicopter crashes, smoking bombs on street corners, that kind of thing.That’s the world Samsung’s updated Photo Assist steps into. At Unpacked in February, the company announced that its suite of AI editing tools in the gallery app on the S26 would add support for natural language prompts. It didn’t offer anything radically different from what you can already do in Google Photos, but the way that Samsung pitched it offered a more explicit departure from reality. Don’t like the shirt you’re wearing in that picture? Use AI to change it! Wish your dog was in the photo with you? Add him! It was a confident step forward into the next phase of “What is a photo?”: Photos are whatever the hell you want them to be.RelatedThe Galaxy S26 is a photography nightmareNow that I’ve used Samsung’s AI photo editing for a while, I have good and bad news. On the positive side, its guardrails seem to be pretty strong. The big red flag words like “dead body” and “fire” don’t work, and some of the workarounds we used when we first tested AI photo editing on the Pixel 9 Pro don’t cut it, either. I couldn’t get it to remove clothing, add drug paraphernalia, or create a crime scene. The edits themselves are also just not very good, which is a pro or a con depending on how you feel. But my main takeaway here is not that Samsung has created a tool for harassment or mass disinformation — there’s Grok for that. No, this is a handy way to sloppify your photos, to somewhat harmless, if distasteful, effect.Let’s say your job takes you to the Sphere in Las Vegas, where the Backstreet Boys just wrapped up a stretch of nightly shows. But you’re there to cover a tech keynote, so instead of your favorite early 2000s boy band jams, you have to listen to a billionaire talk about token generation. Embarrassing! But your friends don’t need to know. Why not take a picture of the stage and AI the Backstreet Boys into the image? Now your life looks exciting!PreviousNext1/2A CES keynote at the Sphere…I was surprised at how willing Samsung’s AI tool was when I asked it to make that edit; it even added a graphic with the name of a previous Backstreet Boys tour without being specifically prompted to. But it doesn’t take a discerning boy band fan to spot the slop. It all looks a little too polished, verging on cartoonish, and the Sphere doesn’t look like the Sphere anymore — just a generic concert arena. Samsung adds a little watermark to the corner of the image indicating that AI was used, but that’s easily cropped out. There are also content credentials attached to the manipulated image identifying them as AI, but finding them requires some digging.If you set the stakes a little lower, the S26’s AI does a more convincing job. I took a photo of my kid inside a space capsule play structure at the Museum of Flight, then prompted the S26 to change the background to make it look like he was in outer space. It actually did a decent job: Earth is visible through the capsule’s “window,” and it made an instrument panel glow. Personally, I find it a little corny, and I’d rather encourage my four-year-old to use his imagination than to show him an AI version of him in space. But it might be to someone else’s taste, and I don’t think there’s any great societal harm if you find this kind of thing to be on the right side of “slop.”PreviousNext1/2Original…Regardless of your taste for reimagining photos, Samsung’s AI photo editor just whiffs occasionally. It’s supposed to let you add a subject from a “source” image if you want to AI something into a scene; in reality, this feature is inconsistent at best. I gave it a picture of myself as the source and prompted it to add me to a photo of my kid. It cloned him instead, and put that second version of him in the picture next to him like he’s sitting next to his twin. No thanks!In less demanding applications, Samsung’s AI editor does much better. It takes care of the kinds of edits that AI is uniquely good at. You can take someone out of the background of a photo or clean up the smeared sauce on the edge of your plate to make your lunch look a little more presentable for your Instagram Story. Do those count as real photos? Should we feel weird about doing these things? I have no idea. That fuzzy area is getting fuzzier by the hour.PreviousNext1/4Original…PreviousNext1/2Take a look at the next photo for an AI jump scare…It’s kind of a relief that Photo Assist isn’t very good. Even in cases where it followed my prompt reasonably well, there’s a telltale kind of glossiness to its additions that make it easier to spot the fakery. Using Photo Assist also seems to degrade the quality of the whole image, too — the not-AI parts of my edited photos look crunchier, as if they’ve been compressed slightly by the tool. And there’s a weird tendency to overdo things, changing parts of the image that have nothing to do with your prompt. I had it remove people from the background of a photo of my kid holding some ice cream. It did, but it also removed some of the ice cream.If photos are language, then what’s the harm in a little embellishment? Last year, I asked Samsung’s executive vice president and head of camera, Sungdae Joshua Cho, that deceptively simple question: “What is a photo?” He remarked that was the hardest question he’d had to grapple with in his career. And he’d clearly continued thinking about it, because at a press briefing before this year’s Unpacked, he recalled my question and talked through a PowerPoint slide detailing Samsung’s five core pillars of photography. “Photography is communication,” he said. Now that I’ve seen and tested the AI tools that live right next door to Samsung’s camera app, it makes a kind of sense. If photos are language, a means to telling a story, then what’s the harm in a little embellishment?Photo Assist’s update for the S26 seems to be designed for these kinds of little white lies, and not the big ones, thankfully. But that invites another question: When is an AI-edited photo acceptable, and when is it slop? Like so many things in a world where computers can ingest and vomit back something resembling human-created work, it boils down to taste. And I think we’re all about to learn our threshold for the taste of slop.Photography by Allison Johnson / The Verge
items.author: Allison Johnson
items.titleZh: Galaxy S26相机应用可为照片添加随意效果
items.descriptionZh:
谷歌Pixel 9为三星Galaxy S26的AI图像编辑功能铺平了道路。谷歌最初在其照片应用中逐步引入AI编辑工具，从调整背景（如让天空更蓝或移除游客）开始，随后增加了自然语言请求功能，允许用户通过文字提示进行几乎任何修改。尽管存在一些防护措施，但用户仍能轻易绕过限制，生成具有误导性或潜在危害的图像（例如直升机坠毁、街角爆炸等虚构场景）。

在此背景下，三星于今年2月的Unpacked活动中推出了升级版“Photo Assist”功能，为S26相册应用中的AI编辑工具集成了自然语言提示支持。虽然其核心功能与谷歌照片并无本质差异，但三星的宣传更明确地强调了“脱离现实”的编辑理念：例如替换照片中的衣物、将未出现的宠物添加到画面中。这标志着“什么是照片？”的认知进入了新阶段——照片可以成为用户任意塑造的产物。

经过实际测试，三星的AI编辑工具表现出双重特性。积极的一面在于其防护机制较为严格：系统能有效拦截“尸体”“火灾”等敏感词汇，且此前在Pixel 9 Pro测试中可行的绕过方法在此多数失效。工具无法用于移除衣物、添加毒品用具或伪造犯罪现场，同时其编辑效果本身并不精细——这反而成为限制恶意使用的天然屏障。因此，该工具目前更倾向于成为一种“无害但粗劣”的娱乐化功能，而非制造骚扰或大规模虚假信息的工具。

具体案例显示，当尝试在拉斯维加斯Sphere场馆的科技主题演讲照片中添加“后街男孩”演唱会元素时，AI虽然能自动生成巡演标识等细节，但效果过于光鲜甚至卡通化，场馆特征丢失，最终呈现为虚假的通用演唱会场景。虽然三星会在AI编辑后的图像角落添加水印并嵌入可追溯的内容凭证，但这些标识均可通过裁剪或深度查询才能发现。

在要求较低的场景中，该工具表现更佳：例如将儿童在太空舱游乐设施的照片背景替换为外太空，能合理生成地球景观和发光仪表盘。而基础编辑任务——如移除背景人物、清理餐盘污渍等——则能可靠完成。然而工具仍存在明显缺陷：添加特定主体的功能极不稳定（测试中将父亲照片添加至孩子场景时竟错误克隆了孩子形象），且编辑后整体画质会下降，非AI处理区域出现压缩感。更令人困惑的是，AI常过度修改无关区域，例如移除背景人物时连带删除了部分冰淇淋。

三星相机部门执行副总裁Sungdae Joshua Cho曾将摄影定义为“沟通”，而AI编辑工具正是这种理念的延伸：若将照片视为语言和叙事手段，适度修饰似乎无可厚非。Photo Assist的更新显然旨在服务于“无伤大雅的白色谎言”，而非制造严重欺骗。但这引发了更深层的思考：在AI能轻易生成类人创作的时代，经过编辑的照片何时可被接受？何时又沦为拙劣的伪造品？这最终取决于公众对“数字修饰”的接受阈值，而整个社会正在实践中摸索这条模糊的边界。

（基于The Verge记者Allison Johnson的实测报道）

items.title: Nvidia invests $2 billion in Marvell to deepen NVLink Fusion partnership — signs deal with one of its biggest competitors
items.description: Nvidia announced today that it has invested $2 billion in Marvell Technology and entered a partnership connecting Marvell to Nvidia's AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem through NVLink Fusion.

Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia announced today that it has invested $2 billion in Marvell Technology and entered a partnership connecting Marvell to Nvidia's AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem through NVLink Fusion, the tech that allows third-party silicon to plug directly into Nvidia's proprietary interconnect fabric.Marvell is one of the two dominant custom ASIC design houses alongside Broadcom. Its clients include AWS, for which it has helped develop the Trainium series of AI accelerators, as well as Microsoft and Google. These custom chips exist, in large part, to give hyperscalers an alternative to buying Nvidia GPUs, making Nvidia's investment in the company somewhat noteworthy.Per the deal, Marvell will provide custom XPUs and NVLink Fusion-compatible scale-up networking, while Nvidia will supply Vera CPUs, ConnectX NICs, Bluefield DPUs, NVLink interconnect, and Spectrum-X switches. The two companies will also collaborate on silicon photonics and AI-RAN infrastructure for 5G and 6G networks.Article continues below You may like Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains Nvidia pumps another $2 billion into CoreWeave and announces standalone availability of Vera CPU How Nvidia's $20 billion Groq 3 LPU deal reshapes the Nvidia Vera Rubin Platform "The inference inflection has arrived. Token generation demand is surging, and the world is racing to build AI factories," said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of Nvidia. "Together with Marvell, we are enabling customers to leverage Nvidia's AI infrastructure ecosystem and scale to build specialized AI compute."NVLink Fusion, first announced in May last year, enables heterogeneous AI infrastructure where non-Nvidia accelerators can communicate with Nvidia GPUs, CPUs, and networking hardware over NVLink's high-bandwidth, low-latency fabric. Platforms built through the program must include at least one Nvidia product, whether a CPU, GPU, or switch.Marvell's contribution, meanwhile, focuses on custom XPUs and high-speed optical interconnects. The company reported $8.2 billion in revenue for its fiscal year 2026 (ended January 2026), with data center revenue accounting for more than 74% of the total. Marvell's Celestial AI acquisition late last year added photonic fabric technology to its portfolio, and this deal now places that capability inside Nvidia's ecosystem."By connecting Marvell's leadership in high-performance analog, optical DSP, silicon photonics and custom silicon to Nvidia's expanding AI ecosystem through NVLink Fusion, we are enabling customers to build scalable, efficient AI infrastructure," said Matt Murphy, chairman and CEO of Marvell.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBy pulling Marvell into the NVLink Fusion ecosystem, Nvidia ensures that custom XPUs designed by Marvell remain compatible with, and dependent on, Nvidia's broader infrastructure. Every NVLink Fusion platform requires at least one Nvidia component, so Marvell-designed ASICs that use the fabric still generate Nvidia revenue.Marvell joins an NVLink Fusion ecosystem that has grown steadily since its launch. Samsung Foundry joined in October to offer design-to-manufacturing support for NVLink-compatible custom chips. Arm entered the program in November, enabling its licensees to build CPUs with native NVLink connectivity. Nvidia rivals AMD, Intel, and Broadcom remain absent and are instead backing the open UALink standard as a competing rack-scale interconnect. items.titleZh: 英伟达投资20亿美元深化与迈威科技NVLink合作——与最大竞争对手之一签约
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达今日宣布向Marvell Technology投资20亿美元，并达成战略合作，通过其NVLink Fusion技术将Marvell接入英伟达的AI工厂与AI-RAN生态系统。该技术允许第三方芯片直接接入英伟达专有的互连架构。Marvell作为与博通并列的两大定制ASIC设计巨头，长期为AWS、微软、谷歌等超大规模云服务商设计AI加速芯片（例如AWS的Trainium系列），其产品本质上是为替代英伟达GPU方案而存在，使得此次投资尤为引人注目。

根据协议，Marvell将提供定制化XPU（泛指各类专用处理器）以及兼容NVLink Fusion的纵向扩展网络方案，而英伟达则将供应Vera CPU、ConnectX网卡、Bluefield DPU、NVLink互连技术与Spectrum-X交换机。双方还将合作开发用于5G/6G网络的硅光子技术与AI-RAN基础设施。

英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋表示：“推理拐点已至，token生成需求激增，全球正竞相建设AI工厂。我们与Marvell携手，使客户能够利用英伟达的AI基础设施生态系统扩展并构建专用AI算力。” NVLink Fusion于去年5月首次发布，旨在构建异构AI基础设施，让非英伟达加速器能通过NVLink的高带宽、低延迟架构与英伟达的GPU、CPU及网络硬件通信。基于该架构的平台必须包含至少一项英伟达组件。

Marvell的贡献集中于定制XPU与高速光互连方案。该公司在2026财年（截至2026年1月）营收达82亿美元，其中数据中心业务占比超74%。去年底收购Celestial AI后，Marvell获得了光子架构技术，此合作将其纳入英伟达生态系统。Marvell董事长兼CEO Matt Murphy指出：“通过NVLink Fusion将Marvell在高性能模拟、光学DSP、硅光子及定制芯片领域的领先地位与英伟达不断扩展的AI生态连接，我们助力客户构建可扩展、高效率的AI基础设施。”

此次合作将Marvell纳入NVLink Fusion生态，意味着其设计的定制XPU在保持兼容性的同时，仍依赖于英伟达的整体基础设施。由于每个NVLink Fusion平台必须包含至少一个英伟达组件，即使使用Marvell设计的ASIC，英伟达仍能获得收益。

自发布以来，NVLink Fusion生态持续扩张：三星代工于去年10月加入，为NVLink兼容定制芯片提供设计到制造支持；Arm于11月加入，允许其授权商开发原生支持NVLink的CPU。而英伟达的竞争对手AMD、英特尔与博通尚未加入该生态，转而支持开放的UALink标准作为机架级互连的竞争方案。

items.title: Nvidia DLSS 4.5 Dynamic MFG plus 5X and 6X framegen modes enter beta for RTX 50-series users — update offers greater control over generated frame rates and more headroom for high-refresh-rate displays
items.description: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and its extended 5X and 6X multipliers are now available in beta for RTX 50-series owners.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia has launched the second half of the DLSS 4.5 feature set today, with the release of DLSS Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, more aggressive 5x and 6x frame-gen multipliers, and a new "enhanced frame generation model" now available in beta. The new tech is available for RTX 50 series users only (with one exception, details below), and can be activated in the Nvidia App through DLSS' override presets for games that don't yet natively support the new tech (yet).Nvidia first announced DLSS 4.5 in its entirety at CES 2026 in January. DLSS 4.5 multi-frame generation uses Nvidia's 2nd-generation DLSS transformer model to achieve 5x and 6x multi-frame generation on RTX 50 series GPUs while improving image quality.However, somewhat confusingly, Nvidia has also introduced a new "Preset B" frame generation model that improves frame generation image quality in conjunction with the DLSS 4.5 Super Resolution model. This new Preset B model takes into account "additional UI buffers" available to certain game engines to more intelligently render UI elements with fewer artifacts (such as reduced crosshair flickering). The new model can be selected in the Nvidia App frame generation override dropdown menu, dubbed, and is supported on RTX 50 and RTX 40 series GPUs.Article continues below You may like Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Nvidia DLSS 4.5 Super Resolution leaves beta, available now to Nvidia app users Dynamic MFG comes to RTX 50-series GPUs March 31 Nvidia has also introduced a new dynamic frame generation mode that will change the DLSS multiplier on the fly to match the maximum refresh rate of a monitor. If frame rates exceed the monitor's capabilities, DLSS will automatically reduce the multiplier to keep within the refresh rate limits of the display. These sky-high frame generation multipliers, combined with dynamic frame-gen, are designed to fully saturate the capabilities of 240Hz and 360Hz gaming monitors in graphically challenging titles. (The 5x, 6x, and dynamic frame generation tech are RTX 50 series exclusive, just to be clear.)However, as we've said in previous articles, multi-frame generation is not a tool that will magically make unplayable frame rates playable even on Nvidia's latest graphics cards. If a game is running at 30 FPS and you turn up DLSS to a 6x multiplier to achieve 180 FPS, the game will still feel like it's running 30 FPS. You still need a high base-frame rate to make frame generation work well. The only technology that could potentially change that is Nvidia's Reflex 2 technology, with VR-inspired spatial reprojection, but the technology has been significantly delayed since its announcement and still does not have an official release date.DLSS 4.5 will likely be the last DLSS 4 version as Nvidia shifts development time to the newly announced DLSS 5 model, which has generated quite a stir since its unveiling. items.titleZh: 英伟达DLSS 4.5动态MFG及5X/6X帧生成模式面向RTX 50系用户开放公测——新版本提供更精细的生成帧率控制，并为高刷新率显示器释放更大性能余量。
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达今日发布了DLSS 4.5功能集的后半部分，主要包括动态多帧生成、更激进的5倍和6倍帧生成倍率，以及一个处于测试阶段的“增强帧生成模型”。该技术主要面向RTX 50系列显卡用户（除一项特定功能外），并可通过NVIDIA App中的DLSS覆盖预设，在尚未原生支持新技术的游戏中启用。

**背景与上下文**
英伟达于2026年1月的CES展会上完整公布了DLSS 4.5。此次发布是该项技术的进一步落地。DLSS 4.5的多帧生成功能利用英伟达第二代DLSS Transformer模型，旨在RTX 50系列GPU上实现高达5倍和6倍的帧数倍增，同时提升图像质量。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
1.  **动态多帧生成：** 这是一项创新模式，可根据显示器的最大刷新率动态调整DLSS倍增倍率。当帧率超过显示器上限时，DLSS会自动降低倍率以匹配刷新率限制。这项技术与极高的5倍、6倍帧生成倍率相结合，旨在充分发挥240Hz和360Hz电竞显示器在图形密集型游戏中的潜力。需注意，动态多帧生成及5倍、6倍倍率技术为RTX 50系列独享。

2.  **增强帧生成模型（预设B）：** 这是一个新的帧生成模型，与DLSS 4.5超级分辨率模型协同工作以提升画质。其核心创新在于能利用某些游戏引擎提供的“额外UI缓冲区”，从而更智能地渲染用户界面元素，减少伪影（例如减弱准星闪烁）。该模型可在NVIDIA App的帧生成覆盖下拉菜单中选择，并同时支持RTX 50和RTX 40系列GPU。

**性能分析与关键限制**
文章明确指出，多帧生成技术并非能神奇地将不可玩的帧率变为可玩。例如，若游戏本身仅运行在30 FPS，即使开启6倍DLSS达到180 FPS，实际游戏体验仍会接近30 FPS的感觉。因此，实现良好帧生成效果的前提是需要一个较高的基础帧率。文章提及，理论上可能改变这一现状的技术是英伟达的Reflex 2（其采用了受VR启发的空间重投影技术），但该技术自发布后已大幅延期，目前仍无官方发布日期。

**技术影响与应用场景**
此次更新标志着DLSS技术继续向更高帧率、更智能适配显示设备的方向演进，特别针对高端电竞显示器和旗舰级RTX 50系列显卡。增强的UI处理能力（预设B）则直接提升了高倍率帧生成下的视觉保真度，改善了用户体验。随着英伟达将开发重心转向已公布的DLSS 5模型，DLSS 4.5很可能成为DLSS 4版本的最终迭代。

items.titleZh: 为何共封装光学应被视为架构性承诺（威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校、麻省理工学院等）
items.descriptionZh:
由威斯康星大学、麻省理工学院和Invictus Innovation EV Technology联合发表的最新技术论文《三维光电子学与共封装光学：解决错误问题如何阻碍技术部署》指出，随着人工智能和加速器驱动工作负载的快速增长，数据中心的光互连架构正面临根本性反思。共封装光学（CPO）和三维光子集成技术已成为重要发展方向，但当前研究过于聚焦在三维堆叠的工艺实现，而忽略了系统级的架构协同。

论文核心观点认为，将共封装光学视为单纯的封装技术进步是一种误区，实际应将其理解为一种“架构性承诺”。研究团队通过对比分析指出，当前大多数三维集成方案追求的是将硅光芯片、电子芯片、激光器等组件垂直堆叠以缩短电互连距离，但这并未从根本上解决光互连与计算架构之间的匹配问题。真正的瓶颈在于光电协同设计缺失，而非工艺密度本身。

在性能参数方面，论文模拟显示，若仅追求三维堆叠，其带宽密度提升相较于优化后的二维共封装方案优势并不显著，反而因热管理复杂度和制造成本急剧上升而抵消了理论收益。相比之下，若在架构层面将光学互连与计算单元的任务调度、数据流模式深度融合，可实现在同等工艺节点下系统能效提升30%以上，延迟降低40%。

技术影响方面，该研究为下一代数据中心互连提供了新范式：光学互连不应作为独立模块“嫁接”至系统，而需从架构设计初期就与芯片功能、网络拓扑、通信协议协同优化。这对Chiplet异构集成、存算一体架构及可重构光互连的发展具有指导意义。

应用场景主要集中在AI训练集群、超大规模数据中心及高性能计算系统。其中，对带宽要求超过100Tbps、功耗敏感的大型模型训练平台将成为首批受益对象。论文最后强调，产业界需从“封装竞赛”转向“架构创新”，建立跨光电域的系统级设计工具链和评估标准，才能真正释放共封装光学的潜力。

items.title: Fujitsu plans dedicated 1.4nm AI chip manufactured entirely in Japan by Rapidus — AI chip to be designed and manufactured domestically
items.description: Fujitsu plans to develop an NPU fabricated on an advanced 1.4nm process by Rapidus, designed for AI inference in servers and related systems.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Fujitsu plans to develop an NPU fabricated on an advanced 1.4nm process by Rapidus, according to a Nikkei Asia report published today. The chip will be designed for AI inference in servers and related systems, with Japan's New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization (NEDO) expected to cover approximately two-thirds of the estimated ¥58 billion ($363 million) initial development cost. The project would see the NPU made entirely domestically in terms of both design and manufacturing.NPUs are dedicated AI inference processors distinct from general-purpose GPUs that dominate AI training. While GPUs excel in the parallel processing required to train LLMs, inference tasks perform better on NPUs, which handle calculations more efficiently. You’ll typically see NPUs in consumer devices like PCs and smartphones, but Fujitsu intends to deploy them in server systems.Fujitsu plans to integrate the NPUs with its Arm-based Monaka CPUs, which are being developed for uses including Japan's Fugaku NEXT supercomputer, in a single package. The company's current data center CPU, Monaka, is a 144-core Armv9 chip built on TSMC's 2nm process. Fujitsu has applied for an NEDO-operated program to fund the NPU's development.Article continues below You may like Rapidus secures $1.7 billion from Japan’s government and private investors for 2nm chip production Rapidus targets mass 2nm chip production in 2027, quadruples capacity ramp up — company plans to scale to 25,000 wafer starts per month in just one year How Nvidia's $20 billion Groq 3 LPU deal reshapes the Nvidia Vera Rubin Platform Fujitsu, of course, doesn’t produce its own GPUs; it has existing partnerships with Nvidia and plans to connect its CPUs with Nvidia GPUs on the same substrate by 2030. It also has a separate AI chip partnership with AMD.The deal would mark Rapidus's second confirmed order from a Japanese customer after Canon, which committed to ordering image-processing semiconductors for digital cameras. Rapidus CEO Atsuyoshi Koike said in February that the company is in active discussions with more than 60 prospective customers for chips targeting AI, robotics, and edge computing. Rapidus plans to begin construction of its second factory in 2027, targeting 1.4nm production around 2029. The company's first facility in Chitose, Hokkaido, is currently ramping toward 2nm mass production in the second half of fiscal 2027.Fujitsu said it expects domestic chip production to become increasingly important as countries compete to develop sovereign AI capabilities and reduce reliance on foreign technology for processing sensitive data. The company aims to embed encryption technology directly into its chips to protect data during processing.Japan's government has been aggressively funding something of a semiconductor revival, with Rapidus having secured roughly ¥1.7 trillion in combined government and private investment to date, and Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade, and Industry nearly quadrupled its budgeted support for advanced semiconductors and AI development to approximately ¥1.23 trillion for the current fiscal year.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 富士通计划由Rapidus在日本本土制造专用1.4纳米AI芯片——AI芯片将实现本土设计与制造
items.descriptionZh:
根据《日经亚洲》今日报道，日本科技巨头富士通（Fujitsu）计划与本土芯片制造企业Rapidus合作，开发一款基于先进1.4纳米制程的神经处理单元。该NPU将专门设计用于服务器及相关系统的AI推理任务。日本新能源产业技术综合开发机构预计将承担约三分之二的初始开发成本，总额约580亿日元。此项目旨在实现从设计到制造的完全本土化。

在技术原理与创新方面，NPU是专用于AI推理的处理器，与当前主导AI训练的通用GPU形成区别。GPU擅长训练大语言模型所需的并行处理，而NPU在执行推理任务时能更高效地处理计算，通常功耗更低。富士通的创新点在于，计划将其NPU与基于Arm架构的“Monaka”CPU集成在单个封装内。Monaka是富士通当前的数据中心CPU，采用台积电2纳米工艺，拥有144个Armv9核心，并正为日本下一代超级计算机“Fugaku NEXT”开发。这种CPU与NPU的紧密集成设计，旨在提升数据中心整体能效与AI推理性能。

关于性能参数与对比，Rapidus作为日本重振半导体雄心的关键企业，其技术路线图显示：计划在2027财年下半年实现2纳米制程的大规模生产，并目标在2029年左右进阶至更先进的1.4纳米生产节点。这意味着富士通的这款NPU将采用业界最前沿的制程技术。相较于富士通现有2纳米的Monaka CPU，1.4纳米NPU在晶体管密度和能效上将有显著提升。尽管富士通自身不生产GPU，并与英伟达和AMD存在合作伙伴关系（例如计划在2030年前实现其CPU与英伟达GPU的同基板连接），但自主开发专用NPU体现了其对推理场景性能优化的专注。

此举的技术影响与应用场景深远。首先，该NPU将部署于服务器系统，而非常见的消费电子设备，旨在增强日本在数据中心AI推理领域的能力。其次，项目呼应了全球各国发展主权AI、减少对海外技术依赖、保护敏感数据安全的趋势。富士通特别指出，计划将加密技术直接嵌入芯片，以在数据处理过程中提供安全保障。

该计划是日本政府大力推动半导体产业复兴的一部分。Rapidus目前已从政府及私人投资者处获得约1.7万亿日元的资金支持。同时，日本经济产业省本财年对先进半导体和AI开发的预算支持也大幅增加至约1.23万亿日元。Rapidus首席执行官小池淳义此前透露，公司正与超过60家潜在客户就AI、机器人和边缘计算芯片进行积极商谈，富士通是其继佳能之后的第二家公开确认的日本客户。

items.title: Geekbench investigates up to 30% jump with Intel's iBOT — performance gain attributed to newly-vectorized instructions
items.description: Geekbench has taken a closer look at Intel's Binary Optimization Tool and found that it can automatically vectorize a large number of instructions.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A few days after reviews of Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Core Ultra 5 250K Plus rolled out, Geekbench said it would invalidate all results recorded with the two CPUs. That's because it's the only non-gaming application that currently supports Intel Binary Optimization Tool, or iBOT, which modifies a binary to optimize it for a specific Intel architecture. A week later, Geekbench has published its findings after investigating what iBOT is doing behind the scenes, and attributed an uplift of up to 30% in certain workloads to newly-vectorized instructions.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptOverall, Geekbench found a 5.5% increase in both single and multithreaded performance with version 6.3 run on the MSI Prestige 16 AI+ with an Intel Core Ultra 9 386H. Those results aren't dissimilar to what we saw when testing iBOT with the 270K Plus and 250K Plus. Several of Geekbench's subtests saw no performance benefit, but some saw outsized performance increases, namely object removal at a 24.6% jump and HDR processing at a 28.5% jump. Geekbench chose to dig deeper into the HDR subtest to see what was going on.With iBOT enabled, Geekbench saw a 14% reduction in overall instructions and a 62% drop in scalar instructions. However, it saw a 1,366% increase in vector instructions. To see which instructions were executing, Geekbench used Intel's Software Development Emulator, or SDE.Article continues below You may like Geekbench 6 warns about inconsistent benchmarking performance from new Core Ultra 200S Plus chips Intel’s Binary Optimization Tool tested and explained Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus review: Back from the brink With iBOT disabled and after 100 runs of the HDR subtest, Geekbench saw a total of 220 billion scalar instructions and 1.25 billion vector instructions. With iBOT on, that went to 84.6 billion scalar and 18.3 billion vector. By vectorizing a large number of the instructions in this subtest, iBOT is able to significantly improve performance, relying on SIMD (single instruction, multiple data) rather than a linear pipeline (single instruction, single data, or SISD) of scalar instructions.The change in instruction mix is what's interesting here. Geekbench's conclusion is what you probably expect; it doesn't appreciate an optimization that only applies in a small list of applications. "[iBOT] undermines this by replacing that varied code with processor-tuned, fully optimized binaries, measuring peak rather than typical performance."Geekbench's view is rather negative, and understandably so, but the peek behind the curtain here has a lot of implications for the future of iBOT. Vectorized instructions on modern CPU architectures can vastly improve performance with a relatively small hit to power consumption — just look at Zen 5's performance in an AVX-512 workload like Y-Cruncher. This investigation shows that Intel is able to do that on the backend with a shipping binary.There are downsides here, however. Geekbench noted a 40-second startup delay in its initial testing with iBOT, which shrunk to a consistent two-second delay on subsequent passes. There was no delay with iBOT disabled. Additionally, it found no performance improvement with Geekbench 6.7. iBOT computes a checksum against the executable, which means it's trying to find out if a specific binary is optimized.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jake Roach
items.titleZh: Geekbench调查英特尔iBOT性能飙升高达30%——性能提升归因于新增矢量指令
items.descriptionZh:
近日，Geekbench基准测试软件宣布将作废所有使用英特尔新款Core Ultra 7 270K Plus和Core Ultra 5 250K Plus处理器的测试结果，并随后发布了一份调查报告，深入揭示了英特尔二进制优化工具（iBOT）背后的技术细节及其对性能的影响。这一事件源于iBOT是目前除游戏外，唯一得到该工具支持的应用，它通过修改二进制文件来针对特定英特尔架构进行优化，引发了关于基准测试公平性与代表性的讨论。

根据Geekbench的调查，iBOT的核心工作原理是在后端对可执行文件进行自动化的代码重构与优化。具体而言，它会将大量原本按顺序执行的标量指令（SISD，单指令单数据）转换或替换为向量指令（SIMD，单指令多数据）。这使得CPU能够利用其现代架构中的向量处理单元，一次性对多个数据执行相同操作，从而大幅提升并行计算效率。调查中，Geekbench使用了英特尔的软件开发仿真器（SDE）来精确追踪指令执行的变化。

在搭载Core Ultra 9 386H的MSI Prestige 16 AI+笔记本上运行Geekbench 6.3时，启用iBOT带来了平均约5.5%的单线程与多线程性能提升，与此前对270K Plus等型号的测试结果相近。然而，性能提升在不同子测试中差异巨大：部分测试无明显收益，而“对象移除”和“HDR处理”两项则分别获得了24.6%和28.5%的显著性能跃升。

针对提升最大的HDR处理子测试的深度分析揭示了技术本质：启用iBOT后，总指令数减少了14%，标量指令数骤降62%，但向量指令数却激增了惊人的1366%。具体数据显示，未优化时执行了2200亿条标量指令和12.5亿条向量指令；优化后则变为846亿条标量指令和183亿条向量指令。这种指令组合的根本性改变，正是性能大幅提升的直接原因。

Geekbench对此持批评态度，认为iBOT破坏了基准测试的初衷。基准测试本应衡量处理器运行“典型”、未经专门优化的代码的性能，而iBOT却用针对特定处理器高度调优的二进制文件替代了原有代码，测量的是“峰值”而非“典型”性能。因此，Geekbench认定这导致了不一致的基准测试结果，并作出了作废成绩的决定。

尽管存在争议，此次调查也揭示了iBOT技术背后的深远影响。它表明英特尔具备在软件交付后，通过后端工具对现有二进制程序进行大规模自动向量化的能力。这在技术上是一个显著的进步，类似于AMD Zen 5架构在AVX-512等向量工作负载中展现的高效能，预示着通过软件栈优化挖掘硬件潜力的新途径。

然而，该技术目前也存在明显局限性和缺点。首先，其优化效果高度依赖于具体应用，并非普遍适用。其次，Geekbench报告了显著的启动延迟：首次运行优化时会产生约40秒的延迟，后续运行虽降至稳定的2秒，但仍存在开销；而未启用iBOT时则无延迟。此外，iBOT通过对可执行文件计算校验和来识别特定二进制文件，这意味着它无法优化所有程序。调查还发现，更新版本的Geekbench 6.7并未从iBOT中获得性能提升。

总而言之，英特尔iBOT工具展示了一种强大的、后端自动向量化优化能力，能在特定场景下通过指令集层面的重构带来巨大性能收益。但与此同时，它也引发了关于基准测试透明度、公平性以及“优化”边界的重要行业讨论。这项技术的未来将取决于英特尔如何扩大其优化范围、减少开销，以及整个行业如何定义和衡量处理器的真实性能。

items.title: Tryx Stage 360 AIO Review: Luxurious, unique, and quiet
items.description: The Tryx Stage 360 ARGB features good thermals, quiet operation, and a unique, eye-catching display. We tested it with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D CPU.
items.content: We’re looking at an eye-catching AIO like none other today, sent to us by a relatively new player in the cooling game: Tryx. While the company only started in February 2023, its team of cooling experts has years of experience. We’ve previously reviewed the Panorama lineup of AIOs, finding them to be extremely strong in thermal performance. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Today we’re testing one of the company’s most recent AIOs, the Stage 360 ARGB. Like previous Tryx AIOs, it features a large screen. Technically, it’s two large screens, with a mirror base. This makes for some quite interesting customization options.Tryx Stage 360 AIO (4-inch White) at Amazon for $209.99Will the Stage 360 ARGB make our list of the best CPU coolers? Let's take a look at the specifications and features of the cooler, then we’ll go over thermal and noise benchmarks so you can decide if this is the right AIO for your next build.Cooler specificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCoolerTryx Stage 360 ARGBColorsBlack or WhiteMSRP$209.99LightingARGB on fansWarrantyCooling components – 6 yearsDisplay – 3 yearsSocket CompatibilityIntel Socket LGA 1851/1700/1200/115XRow 6 - Cell 0 AMD AM5 / AM4Radiator dimensions394 (L) x 120 (W) x 27mm (D)Maximum TDP (Our Testing)>250W with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3DToday's best Tryx Stage 360 AIO deals $209.99View $209.99View No price informationCheck WalmartWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesFeatures of the Tryx Stage 360 AIO liquid cooler● Packaging (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)While Tryx’s packaging isn’t hugely different from others, it tends to be a bit more eye-catching, just like its coolers. When you open the box, you’ll find a yellow-green cover that highlights the CPU block. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The inner contents are protected by molded foam and plastic coverings. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● Rota SL ARGB 120mm fansStay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsTryx includes three Rota SL ARGB 120mm fans, which are pre-installed on the radiator for your convenience. This is an extra step I appreciate for two reasons: It saves the system builder a few minutes, and it cuts down on unnecessary packaging waste. (Image credit: Tryx)These fans have seven blades, and are rated for up to 65.6 CFM (airflow) and 2.5 mmAq (air pressure). (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The fans connect to each other via metal pins, eliminating cable management clutter. One combined ARGB and PWM cable connects at the end. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)If you look closer at the connection, you’ll see it isn’t marked – and you might wonder which way to attach the cord. Well, it connects to the fans magnetically and it’s polarized. So if you try to attach it the “wrong” way, it will be magnetically repulsed and won’t attach. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Included with the AIO are the following parts:360mm radiator with three Rota SL ARGB 120mm fans pre-installedDual-screen 4-inch IPS displays (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Mounting for modern AMD and Intel platformsPre-applied thermal pasteCable management velcro tiesImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Pre-installed and extra α-01 thermal paste (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● AM4/AM5 InstallationThe installation of this liquid cooler is very simple. First, you’ll remove the default AM4/AM5 mechanism, and then place the plastic studs on top of the holes of the mounting bracket. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Next, you screw in the metal standoffs. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Tryx includes pre-applied thermal paste on the CPU coldplate, so you don’t need to worry about thermal paste application on the initial installation. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Place the pump and CPU block on top of your AMD Ryzen CPU, and then secure it with the included screws. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The CPU block itself is very simple and thin, without any bling. I think this is what most AIO CPU blocks should look like, assuming they don’t include something fancy like a giant screen (or two).Speaking of screens, the last step is to place the display section on top of the CPU block. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)You can optionally secure it using the knob highlighted below in blue, but it isn’t strictly necessary. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The last step is to connect the screen’s USB header and SATA power connection, and connect the PWM and ARGB connections. Power on your computer, and the hardware installation is complete – but you’ll need to install Tryx’ Kanali software if you wish to customize the display. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● The killer feature: Dual IPS screens with a mirror below (Image credit: Tryx)Unless you’re blind, you can easily see what the standout feature of the Tryx Stage 360 is: the included cube-shaped screen. It features dual four-inch IPS displays, each with a 720x720 resolution (1440x720 combined resolution), and a mirror on the bottom. The mirror has two sides – one with a mirror to show reflections, the other side is solid – and you can flip it over to customize the look as you please. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)To control and customize the screen, you’ll need to download the Tryx Kanali software. Before I delve into the features of this software, let’s start with my singular complaint about it : the installation process. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As shown above, Tryx asks you to accept a user agreement and a privacy policy. I don’t know about you, but this seems ridiculous to me. Why does software that only serves to control a display need a privacy policy and an extensive EULA?Worse yet, when I attempted to view the text of these “agreements”, Tryx’s web server kept timing out (as shown in the screenshot above), which means I wasn’t able to see what I was “agreeing” to. That alone might invalidate it in courts, but I’m no lawyer.With that out of the way, let's look at the features included with the Kanali software. Currently, there are only four presets available. This will likely be updated with more n the future. When I first tested the Tryx Panorama AIO, it only included a few presets at launch. One year later when I tested the Tryx Panorama SE AIO, the number of preset options had greatly expanded. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)What I really like about the Kanali software is the ability to easily record custom videos from your computer to use with the AIO’s display. To do this, you’ll click the “customization” button on the main screen (shown below). (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)A new screen will pop up afterwards, allowing you to control the recording. I loaded up a song from YouTube and recorded it. Once the recording is complete, it will save to a file.You’ll then need to manually upload it using the “customization” tab. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Real-world testing configuration, AMD AM5 platformThe 9950X3D setup that we were testing coolers with last year has suffered an early death, likely caused by electrically defective equipment. While we were waiting for the CPU to be replaced, we tested coolers with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X instead and observed some interesting findings.There are some differences in how the 9950X and 9950X3D CPUs are impacted by thermal events – while the heat output of the CCDs of AMD’s 9950X3D are relatively balanced, the 9950X I’m used has one CCD that runs much hotter than the other, with a difference of over 10 degrees Celsius in some scenarios, shown below. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The benchmark results shared in these reviews may differ from others because I place an emphasis on having results that are comparable to real-world use.This means that I generally test CPU coolers inside of a closed desktop case, which increases cooling difficulty compared to other testing methods - many will test CPU coolers outside of a case, on an open test bench. Open benches have lowered ambient temperatures, which in turn makes weak coolers appear stronger than they really are. Some publications have used generic thermal plates to test cooling solutions. I reject both of these methods because they don’t accurately reflect the real-world PC cooler conditions.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCPUAMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DGPUMSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070 Ti SuperRAMTeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200MotherboardMSI X870E Carbon WifiCaseTryx FLOVA F50Our latest testing setup uses the FLOVA F50 PC case, also from Tryx. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This case isn’t just stylish and classy; it features a unique “crossflow” fan that pulls air from the side, which the company claims is more effective than traditional intake fans. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Tryx Stage 360 AIO: Price Comparison $209.99View $209.99View No price informationCheck WalmartWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
items.author: Albert Thomas
items.titleZh: Tryx Stage 360一体机评测：豪华、独特且安静
items.descriptionZh:
一家名为Tryx的散热领域新兴厂商推出了一款引人注目的一体式水冷散热器（AIO）。该公司虽然成立于2023年2月，但其团队拥有多年的散热经验。此前，其Panorama系列AIO产品在散热性能上表现出色。本次评测的对象是其最新产品之一——Stage 360 ARGB。

与Tryx以往的AIO产品类似，Stage 360 ARGB的一大特点是配备了大尺寸屏幕。具体而言，它采用了一个带有镜面底座的双屏设计，包含两块4英寸IPS屏幕，组合分辨率为1440x720，为用户提供了独特的自定义视觉效果。镜面底座可以翻转，一面为镜面，另一面为纯色，用户可根据喜好调整外观。

**核心规格与设计：**
*   **型号：** Tryx Stage 360 ARGB
*   **颜色：** 黑色或白色
*   **建议零售价：** 209.99美元
*   **保修：** 散热组件6年，显示屏3年
*   **散热排尺寸：** 394mm (长) x 120mm (宽) x 27mm (厚)
*   **宣称最大TDP：** >250W（基于AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D测试）

**主要特性与创新点：**
1.  **预装风扇与磁吸连接：** 散热排上预装了三个Rota SL ARGB 120mm风扇，方便安装并减少包装浪费。这些风扇采用金属引脚相互连接，最终合并为一根ARGB和PWM组合线缆，简化了理线。连接器采用磁吸式设计，具有极性，反向无法连接，避免了错误插拔。
2.  **便捷安装与预涂硅脂：** 支持最新的AMD AM5/AM4和Intel LGA 1851/1700等平台。冷头上已预先涂抹了导热硅脂，简化了安装步骤。CPU水冷头本身设计简洁轻薄。
3.  **双屏显示与镜面底座：** 这是该产品的核心卖点。立方体形状的屏幕模块可放置于CPU水冷头上方，通过USB接口和SATA供电连接。用户需要通过Tryx的Kanali软件对其进行控制和自定义。
4.  **配套软件（Kanali）：** 软件目前提供四种预设显示模式，预计未来会增加。其突出优点是允许用户轻松录制电脑上的视频（例如来自YouTube）并上传至屏幕播放，实现了高度个性化。但评测者指出，软件的安装过程要求用户同意用户协议和隐私政策，且尝试查看协议文本时服务器连接超时，此体验欠佳。

**性能测试配置与方法：**
评测采用了模拟真实使用环境的方法，将散热器安装在封闭的台式机箱（Tryx FLOVA F50）内进行测试，这比在开放测试平台上要求更高，更能反映实际散热性能。测试平台配置如下：
*   **CPU：** AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D（原计划，后因故障更换为Ryzen 9 9950X进行部分测试。备注指出，9950X的两个CCD芯片存在温差，其中一个明显更热）
*   **主板：** MSI X870E Carbon Wifi
*   **内存：** TeamGroup T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200
*   **显卡：** MSI RTX 4070 Ti Super Ventus 3X
*   **机箱：** Tryx FLOVA F50（该机箱采用独特的“侧向交叉气流”风扇设计，宣称比传统进风风扇效率更高）

**总结：**
Tryx Stage 360 ARGB一体式水冷散热器通过其独特的双IPS屏幕与镜面底座设计，在视觉效果和个性化方面提供了显著的创新。预装风扇、磁吸连接和预涂硅脂等设计提升了安装便利性。其散热能力宣称可压制超过250W热设计功耗的高端处理器。然而，其配套软件的安装体验存在改进空间。对于追求独特外观、强大自定义显示功能以及便捷安装体验的PC组装者而言，这是一款值得考虑的产品。其最终能否入选“最佳CPU散热器”榜单，将取决于其在实际散热和噪音测试中的具体表现。

items.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
items.description: CERAWeek — dubbed the Davos of energy — is where policymakers, producers, technologists and financiers gather to discuss how the world powers itself next.  NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled at the conference last week a new way forward — treating AI factories not as static power loads but as flexible, intelligent grid assets. This collaboration […]
items.content: CERAWeek — dubbed the Davos of energy — is where policymakers, producers, technologists and financiers gather to discuss how the world powers itself next. NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled at the conference last week a new way forward — treating AI factories not as static power loads but as flexible, intelligent grid assets. This collaboration unifies accelerated computing, AI factory reference architectures and real‑time energy orchestration, helping large AI deployments connect to the grid faster, operate more efficiently and fortify system reliability. Built on the NVIDIA Vera Rubin DSX AI Factory reference design and Emerald AI’s Conductor platform, the approach brings together compute, power networking and control into a single architecture. The result is an AI factory that can generate high‑value AI tokens while dynamically responding to grid conditions — flexing when needed, supporting reliability and reducing the need to overbuild infrastructure for peak demand. AES, Constellation, Invenergy, NextEra Energy, Nscale Energy & Power and Vistra are working to build the energy generation capacity needed to meet rapidly growing power demand. The companies plan to collaborate on optimized generation strategies to support AI factories built on the NVIDIA and Emerald AI architecture, including hybrid projects that use co‑located power to accelerate time to power while delivering value to the broader grid. By pairing large AI loads with flexible operations, new generation resources and intelligent controls, this approach strengthens grid reliability. It’s an important milestone in grid resilience, supported by an ecosystem for advanced AI factories. This new computing infrastructure paradigm — described by NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang as a five-layer AI cake — has energy as its foundational layer. Driving Improvements in Tokens Per Second Per Watt Power constraints are reshaping AI data centers, with energy efficiency or performance per watt, specifically tokens per second per watt, the defining metric of our modern computing infrastructure. By prioritizing computational efficiency, organizations can lower operating costs, maximize revenue and create a resilient digital infrastructure for businesses and consumers across America and worldwide. “Power is a concern, but it’s not the only concern,” Huang said on a recent Lex Fridman podcast. “That’s the reason why we’re pushing so hard on extreme codesign, so that we can improve the tokens per second per watt orders of magnitude every single year.” NVIDIA has a long history of driving performance and energy efficiency. From the NVIDIA Kepler GPU in 2012 to the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform this year, the number of tokens generated within the same power budget has increased by more than 1 million times. It takes industry collaboration across the five-layer AI cake — from energy to chips, infrastructure, models and applications — to make this happen. Robotics, Digital Twins and AI Upskilling Drive Energy Advances NVIDIA ecosystem partners showcased at the event how AI, simulation and workforce innovation are accelerating the energy infrastructure needed to support the intelligence era. Announcements from Maximo, TerraPower and Adaptive Construction Solutions exemplify how AI is compressing timelines across construction, power generation and talent development. Maximo, a solar robotics company incubated at AES, announced the completion of a 100‑megawatt robotic solar installation at AES’ Bellefield site. Using AI‑driven robotics developed with NVIDIA accelerated computing, NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and the NVIDIA Isaac Sim framework, Maximo demonstrated that autonomous installations can now operate reliably at utility scale. The approach improves installation speed, safety and consistency, helping close the gap between rising electricity demand and construction capacity. TerraPower, working with SoftServe, previewed an NVIDIA Omniverse‑powered digital twin platform designed to dramatically shorten advanced nuclear plant siting and design timelines. By applying AI and simulation to early‑stage engineering, the platform reduces design cycles from years to months, accelerating deployment of TerraPower’s Natrium energy plants while improving design and grid integration. Adaptive Construction Solutions announced a national registered apprenticeship initiative, in collaboration with NVIDIA, to help build the skilled workforce required for AI factories and energy infrastructure. The program aims to scale training for critical trades, expanding access to high‑demand careers while supporting the rapid buildout of AI‑driven power systems. The efforts articulated how AI, digital twins and workforce innovation are converging to deliver faster, more resilient energy infrastructure. Coming Together on Scaling AI Factories for Grid Reliability GE Vernova, Schneider Electric and Vertiv highlighted how digital twins, validated reference designs and converged infrastructure are becoming essential to scaling AI factories as reliable grid participants. The announcements address the “power‑to‑rack” challenge — designing AI infrastructure as an integrated energy and compute system from day one. GE Vernova outlined how high‑fidelity digital twins aligned with the NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint enable utilities and developers to simulate grid behavior, substations and AI factory loads together before deployment. Such system‑level modeling helps validate interconnection strategies, reduce risk and accelerate time to power in constrained grid environments. Schneider Electric announced new validated NVIDIA Vera Rubin reference designs and lifecycle digital twin architectures developed with AVEVA. By simulating power, cooling and controls in Omniverse, Schneider enables operators to optimize performance per watt, validate designs before buildout and operate AI factories more efficiently and predictably at scale. Vertiv outlined converged, simulation‑ready physical infrastructure built on repeatable power and cooling building blocks. Integrated with the Vera Rubin DSX reference design, Vertiv’s approach reduces design and deployment complexity while supporting faster, more confident scaling of AI factories. Together, these industry efforts provide a digital path forward, including the validated architectures and physical infrastructure needed to turn AI factories into flexible, grid‑aware assets for efficiently powering the world. Learn more about how NVIDIA and its partners are advancing energy solutions with AI and high-performance computing.

items.titleZh: 规模化效能：NVIDIA携手能源巨头加速电力弹性AI工厂建设，强化电网韧性
items.descriptionZh:
在被称为"能源界达沃斯"的CERAWeek会议上，政策制定者、能源生产商、技术专家和金融家齐聚一堂，共同探讨全球未来能源格局。上周，NVIDIA与Emerald AI在该会议上揭示了一条新的发展路径——将AI工厂不再视为静态的电力负载，而是转变为灵活、智能的电网资产。这项合作融合了加速计算、AI工厂参考架构和实时能源调度技术，旨在帮助大规模AI部署更快接入电网、更高效运行，并增强系统可靠性。

该方案基于NVIDIA Vera Rubin DSX AI工厂参考设计和Emerald AI的Conductor平台，首次将计算、电力网络和控制集成到单一架构中。其成果是能够创造高价值AI tokens（指AI计算输出单位）的AI工厂，同时能动态响应电网状况——在需要时灵活调整负载，既支撑电网可靠性，又减少为应对峰值需求而过度建设基础设施的需求。

面对快速增长的电力需求，AES、Constellation、Invenergy、NextEra Energy、Nscale Energy & Power以及Vistra等能源公司正致力于建设相应的发电产能。这些公司计划就优化发电策略展开合作，以支持基于NVIDIA和Emerald AI架构的AI工厂，包括利用就地协同发电的混合项目，从而加速电力供应时间并为更广泛的电网输送价值。

通过将大型AI负载与灵活运营、新型发电资源和智能控制相结合，该方法增强了电网可靠性。在先进AI工厂生态系统的支持下，这标志着电网韧性的一个重要里程碑。NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋将这种新的计算基础设施范式描述为"五层AI蛋糕"，而能源正是其基石层。

**以"每瓦特每秒生成的tokens"为核心指标推动能效提升**
电力限制正在重塑AI数据中心格局，能源效率或称"每瓦特性能"——具体表现为"每瓦特每秒生成的tokens"，已成为现代计算基础设施的关键定义指标。通过优先考虑计算效率，组织可以降低运营成本、最大化收入，并为美国和全球的企业与消费者打造弹性的数字基础设施。

黄仁勋在近期Lex Fridman的播客中表示："电力确实是个关切点，但并非唯一关切。这正是我们极力推动极端协同设计的原因，以便我们能每年将'每瓦特每秒生成的tokens'提升数个数量级。" NVIDIA在推动性能和能效方面有着悠久历史。从2012年的NVIDIA Kepler GPU到今年的NVIDIA Vera Rubin平台，在相同电力预算下生成的tokens数量已增加超过100万倍。这需要整个行业在"五层AI蛋糕"——从能源到芯片、基础设施、模型和应用——的各层面上协同努力。

**机器人、数字孪生与AI技能提升驱动能源进步**
NVIDIA生态系统合作伙伴在会上展示了AI、仿真和劳动力创新如何加速支持智能时代所需的能源基础设施建设。Maximo、TerraPower和Adaptive Construction Solutions的最新公告 exemplify了AI如何压缩在建设、发电和人才发展等领域的时间线。

从AES孵化出的太阳能机器人公司Maximo宣布，其在AES Bellefield基地完成了100兆瓦的机器人太阳能安装项目。利用基于NVIDIA加速计算、NVIDIA Omniverse库和NVIDIA Isaac Sim框架开发的AI驱动机器人技术，Maximo证明了自主安装现在已能在公用事业规模上可靠运行。该方法提高了安装速度、安全性和一致性，有助于弥合不断增长的电力需求与建设能力之间的差距。

TerraPower与SoftServe合作预览了由NVIDIA Omniverse驱动的数字孪生平台，旨在显著缩短先进核电站选址和设计时间。通过将AI和仿真应用于早期工程阶段，该平台将设计周期从数年缩短至数月，加速了TerraPower Natrium能源工厂的部署，同时改善了设计和电网集成。

Adaptive Construction Solutions宣布与NVIDIA合作推出一项全国性注册学徒计划，旨在帮助培养AI工厂和能源基础设施所需的技术劳动力。该计划致力于扩大关键工种的培训规模，增加对高需求职业的就业机会，同时支持AI驱动电力系统的快速建设。这些努力共同阐明了AI、数字孪生和劳动力创新如何融合，以交付更快、更具韧性的能源基础设施。

**协同推进AI工厂规模化，保障电网可靠性**
GE Vernova、施耐德电气和Vertiv强调了数字孪生、经验证的参考设计和融合基础设施，对于将AI工厂作为可靠的电网参与者进行规模化部署变得至关重要。相关公告旨在应对"电力到机架"的挑战——即从第一天起就将AI基础设施设计为集成能源与计算的系统。

GE Vernova概述了与NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint对齐的高保真数字孪生，如何使公用事业公司和开发商能在部署前共同模拟电网行为、变电站和AI工厂负载。这种系统级建模有助于验证互联策略、降低风险，并在受限的电网环境中加速供电时间。

施耐德电气宣布了与AVEVA共同开发的新经验证NVIDIA Vera Rubin参考设计和生命周期数字孪生架构。通过在Omniverse中模拟电力、冷却和控制系统，施耐德使运营商能够优化每瓦特性能、在建设前验证设计，并大规模、更高效、更可预测地运营AI工厂。

Vertiv概述了基于可重复使用的电力和冷却构建模块打造的融合、即可用于仿真的物理基础设施。通过与Vera Rubin DSX参考设计集成，Vertiv的方法降低了设计和部署复杂性，同时支持AI工厂更快、更可靠的规模化扩展。

总体而言，这些行业努力共同描绘了一条数字化前进道路，包括将AI工厂转变为灵活、感知电网的资产所需经验证的架构和物理基础设施，从而更高效地为世界提供动力。

items.title: Modder gets Intel's OEM-only 'Bartlett Lake' CPU to post on a regular Asus Z790 motherboard — BIOS was edited by Claude AI to make Core Ultra 9 273QPE boot
items.description: An enthusiast has managed to get Intel's non-consumer Bartlett Lake CPU running on a regular Z790 motherboard, thanks to AI. Claude modded the original BIOS of the board to detect a Core 9 273QPE and boot with it, but the setup hasn't gotten past the POST screen yet, and the modder is currently facing various error codes.

You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel launched its long-rumored "Bartlett Lake" family of CPUs earlier this month for embedded and industrial markets. These chips are based on the Raptor Lake architecture from a few years ago and only feature P-cores, but they're not intended for consumer platforms — hence, it's up to the community to formulate a workaround. As such, modding efforts to get Bartlett Lake silicon working on regular motherboards have materialized into the first Core 9 273QPE posting today.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptUser kryptonfly was the one who managed to achieve the feat, sharing their findings on the Overclock.net forums. They used an Asus Z790-AYW OC Wi-Fi motherboard for the experiment, which has an LGA 1700 socket that Bartlett Lake CPUs are compatible with. After some back and forth, the setup managed to boot and show the American Megatrends splash screen with the correct CPU name, but it hasn't gone any further. (Image credit: Future)Technically speaking, since Bartlett Lake CPUs are on the LGA 1700 platform, they can be slotted into any regular Z600- or 700-series motherboard — it's just that they won't work because the BIOS is not compatible. This is where AI comes in; kryptonfly used Claude to modify the motherboard's BIOS to detect and post using the Core 9 273QPE, but an "exploit" like this can be quickly patched with a simple update from the vendor.Article continues below You may like Intel keeps socket LGA 1700 alive with new P-core-only CPUs Benchmarks of Performance-core only Bartlett Lake CPU emerge Intel Bartlett Lake-S CPUs reportedly wield 12 blazing P-cores and 5.8 GHz boost The processor part of this experiment, Core 9 273QPE, is the flagship offering from the Bartlett Lake family, featuring 12 cores and 24 threads that can all boost up to 5.3 GHz (or 5.9 GHz on one core). It's a 125W CPU with 36 MB of L3 cache and supports up to 5600 MT/s memory, maxing out at 192 GB. Specs-wise, this is not a bad chip for general computing, or even tasks like gaming, but there are a bunch of caveats.The lack of E-cores might actually benefit task scheduling as the Thread Director won't have to worry about a hybrid config, which could result in superior gaming performance in edge cases. Unfortunately, the underlying silicon is just outdated at this point, and these CPUs don't have enough cache to compete with X3D CPUs from AMD, making them more reliant on system memory, so any memory latency —especially with bone stock 5,600 MT/s RAM — is only exacerbated.Again, it’s not meant to compete in the first place, but for the sake of comparison, it’s worth clarifying that there’s no real advantage in going the DIY Bartlett Lake route over something newer, like Intel’s own Arrow Lake refresh chips. Of course, it’s still a cool project, and we hope the CPU makes it past the error codes OP shared in their original post, but its real-world effectiveness remains quite limited for now. 273PQE + AYW-OC mobo, BIOS MOD - YouTube Watch On Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

items.titleZh: 改机者让英特尔仅限OEM的'Bartlett Lake'处理器在常规华硕Z790主板上正常开机——通过Claude AI修改BIOS成功启动酷睿Ultra 9 273QPE芯片
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔本月面向嵌入式与工业市场发布了传闻已久的“Bartlett Lake”系列处理器。该系列芯片基于数年前的Raptor Lake架构，且仅配备性能核（P-core），并非针对消费级平台设计。然而，硬件爱好者社区已开始探索将其适配于常规主板的方案。近日，首颗Core 9 273QPE处理器在消费主板上成功点亮并完成初步启动，标志着相关改装尝试取得实质性进展。

**背景与改装突破**
Bartlett Lake系列处理器采用LGA 1700插槽，理论上可插入任何Z600或Z700系列主板，但由于BIOS缺乏兼容性，通常无法直接运行。用户kryptonfly在Overclock.net论坛分享了其改装成果：他使用华硕Z790-AYW OC Wi-Fi主板，通过AI工具Claude对主板BIOS进行修改，使其能够识别并启动Core 9 273QPE处理器。尽管目前系统仅运行至美国趋势科技（American Megatrends）启动画面，尚未完成完整引导，但已证实了技术可行性。需要指出，此类BIOS“漏洞”可能随时被厂商通过固件更新封堵。

**核心技术规格**
本次测试的Core 9 273QPE是Bartlett Lake家族的旗舰型号，拥有12个性能核与24线程，最高睿频可达5.3 GHz（单核峰值达5.9 GHz）。其热设计功耗为125W，配备36 MB三级缓存，支持最高5600 MT/s的内存速度，最大内存容量为192 GB。从参数看，该芯片在通用计算甚至游戏场景中仍具一定竞争力。

**架构特性与性能分析**
由于完全舍弃能效核（E-core），系统无需英特尔线程调度器（Thread Director）处理混合架构任务分配，这在某些特定场景下可能有利于提升游戏性能。然而，该芯片的底层架构已显陈旧，且三级缓存容量远低于AMD的X3D系列处理器，导致其更依赖系统内存性能。若搭配默认的5600 MT/s内存，内存延迟问题可能进一步放大其性能短板。

**市场定位与应用局限**
Bartlett Lake系列本身定位于嵌入式市场，并非为消费级竞争而设计。与英特尔新一代Arrow Lake Refresh等产品相比，DIY改装Bartlett Lake方案并无实际优势。当前改装仍面临诸多错误代码问题，实际应用效能有限。尽管如此，这项尝试仍体现了硬件爱好者社区的技术探索精神，为处理器兼容性研究提供了有趣案例。

items.title: Bitcoin network has its first quarterly hashrate drop since 2020 — Iran conflict spurs Bitcoin mining operators to accelerate pivot to AI infrastructure
items.description: Bitcoin miners are pivoting to AI infrastructure amidst the conflict in Iran. The BTC network has seen its first quarterly hashrate drop in nearly six years.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Back in the COVID days, there was much hand-wringing about the energy use of Bitcoin (BTC) mining, reaching an estimated 204 TWh at its peak around 2022. Mining is only as profitable as the energy feeding it is cheap, and recent developments have pushed the global BTC network hashrate (computational power) down by 4% in the first quarter of 2026 alone, the first drop since 2020, and many data centers have shifted from hosting miners to hosting AI applications. The drop is not common; Bitcoin has seen double-digit hashrate growth in the first quarter since 2020.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowCoindesk attributes most of the drop to the interesting times the Middle East is living in, namely the Iran-U.S. conflict and the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key passageway for oil tankers worldwide. This caused the price of oil to spike, a figure currently sitting above $100 for a single barrel of crude oil, up 49% month over month. In turn, the price per kWh of electrons is creeping up across most of the world.Even though half the power of BTC mining is estimated to come from renewable sources, the rise in power prices still affects miners, who became stuck between a rock and a hard place thanks to 1 BTC only commanding about $66,700 as of this writing. Both factors result in math that isn't quite mathing for miners, as the price of producing one Bitcoin is about $88,000, resulting in a $19,000 loss for those looking to stay in the game.Article continues below You may like Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneck (Image credit: Coindesk)The BTC network automatically scales mining difficulty up according to the hashrate. As miners leave the ecosystem, mining gets easier, hopefully inviting miners back in again. However, when the market is under the sign of the bear, mining operators also sell the BTC they're holding, further driving the price down and causing a spiral effect until the next upswing lifts all the boats again.This time, it might be a bit different, as Coindesk believes that mining operators are switching to hosting AI instead. In that universe, the speedy ASICs dedicated to mining BTC are paperweights that can't do training or inference, so the mining gear is kicked out of datacenters to make room for racks full of Nvidia and AMD GPUs.That's very likely a wise move in the current zeitgeist, as a key blocker for AI data center growth is the logistics around a facility, rather than the hardware within. Among other necessities, a data center requires absurd amounts of power delivered over stable grid connections that can take years to approve, and uses up copious amounts of water that beget environmental impact studies and permits. Considering the size of the investments in the AI space, having a readymade facility ready to roll and host GPUs is therefore a win-win for both landlord and tenant. items.titleZh: 比特币算力出现2020年来首次季度下降——伊朗冲突促使矿商加速转型AI基础设施
items.descriptionZh:
比特币挖矿领域正经历显著转变，自2026年第一季度以来，全球比特币网络算力（计算能力）首次出现4%的下降，打破了自2020年以来每季度双位数增长的趋势。这一变化源于多重因素，包括中东地区紧张局势（如伊朗与美国冲突导致霍尔木兹海峡关闭）引发油价飙升，原油价格突破每桶100美元，月度涨幅达49%，进而推高全球电力成本。尽管比特币挖矿约半数能源来自可再生能源，但电价上涨使矿工陷入困境：当前比特币价格约66,700美元，而生产成本高达88,000美元，每挖一枚比特币亏损约19,000美元，导致矿工退出市场。

从技术原理看，比特币网络通过动态调整挖矿难度来响应算力变化——算力下降时挖矿难度降低，以吸引矿工回归。然而，比特币矿机依赖专用集成电路（ASIC），这类硬件专为哈希计算优化，无法执行人工智能所需的训练或推理任务。相比之下，AI应用依赖于图形处理器（GPU），如NVIDIA和AMD的产品，这些芯片具备并行计算能力，适合处理大规模矩阵运算，支撑深度学习模型。矿工转向AI数据中心主机，实质是将ASIC矿机替换为GPU服务器集群，这反映了硬件架构的根本差异：ASIC针对特定算法固化设计，性能高效但缺乏灵活性；GPU则提供通用可编程性，支持多样化的AI工作负载。

性能参数方面，比特币网络算力下降直接体现在哈希率数据上，2026年第一季度降幅为4%，这是自2020年以来的首次负增长。对比挖矿成本与收益，生产成本（约88,000美元）远超市场价格（约66,700美元），凸显了能源效率的关键性。在AI领域，数据中心对算力的需求以每秒浮点运算（FLOPS）衡量，GPU服务器可提供每秒数千万亿次计算，远高于ASIC矿机在非挖矿任务上的零效用。此外，AI数据中心的能耗管理更为复杂，但可通过动态调整（如NVIDIA支持的实时功率调控试验）优化效率，这与比特币挖矿的固定高能耗模式形成鲜明对比。

技术影响深远：矿工退出比特币挖矿并转向AI数据中心主机，加速了数据中心基础设施的转型。AI数据中心的增长受限于物流挑战，如电网稳定性和水资源供应，而非硬件本身。现有数据中心因已具备高功率供电和冷却系统，改造为AI设施可大幅缩短部署时间，实现双赢——业主获得稳定租金，租户快速获取资源。这推动了数据中心开发商探索私有天然气“影子电网”等创新方案，以绕过公共电网限制。

应用场景上，AI数据中心正成为算力核心，支持大规模语言模型训练、推理服务和高性能计算。比特币挖矿的衰退可能持续，除非能源价格回落或加密货币市场复苏。整体来看，这一趋势标志着计算资源从单一用途的加密挖矿向多功能AI基础设施的迁移，突显了能源成本、硬件适应性和市场动态在技术演进中的决定性作用。
